title: Textual Inversion of Motion Diffusion Models as an Editing Primitive
teaser: Given a 3D human motion sequence, recover an editable text-space embedding inside a frozen motion diffusion model — then swap per-body-part tokens between motions to produce compositional, body-part-level edits.
abstract:
Modern text-to-motion diffusion models learn a rich distribution over human motion conditioned on language. This project asks: can we invert that relationship? Given a single motion sequence and a frozen text-to-motion diffusion model (MDM), optimize a text-space embedding that best explains the motion in the model's own learned prior — then use that embedding as a body-part-level editing primitive.

The approach exploits SMPL's body-part structure by parameterising the inverted embedding as a grid of per-kinematic-chain × per-temporal-segment tokens, with a CLIP-vocabulary-anchor regularizer to fight memorization. Once inverted, individual chain tokens (e.g., the left-arm group) can be swapped between motions to produce compositional body-part-level edits while keeping the rest of the body fixed.

This is the first study of textual inversion in the SMPL motion domain. Pixel-space inversion (Reenact Anything, SIGGRAPH 2025) and trained motion captioners (MotionGPT-3) cannot offer this kind of editable, kinematic-chain-aware embedding handle.
milestones: Set up the environment, reproduce MDM on HumanML3D, and verify DDIM noise inversion reconstructs motion to within MPJPE < 30 mm.
Implement SMPL-aware textual inversion that optimises a grid of per-kinematic-chain × per-temporal-segment tokens against the frozen MDM with a CLIP-vocabulary-anchor regularizer.
Run the chain-disentanglement probe to quantify spillover between body-part token groups and tune the token-grid dimensions.
Build the body-part editing demo by swapping per-chain inverted tokens between two motions and producing 8–12 demo videos.
Evaluate reconstruction quality (MPJPE, FID, R-Precision) and editing quality (per-chain spillover MPJPE) against MotionCLR attention editing and SimMotionEdit baselines.

prerequisites.label: Advanced Deep Learning for Computer Vision: Visual Computing (ADL4CV)

references.label: An Image is Worth One Word — Textual Inversion (Gal et al., ICLR 2023)
Reenact Anything — Motion-Conditioned Video Generation via Motion-Textual Inversion (SIGGRAPH 2025)
MDM — Human Motion Diffusion Model (Tevet et al.)
Null-text Inversion for Editing Real Images (Mokady et al., CVPR 2023)
HumanML3D — Generating Diverse and Natural 3D Human Motions from Text (CVPR 2022)
